entry.title: Build, Deploy, and Extend AI Agents with Joule Studio
entry.content:
Build, Deploy, and Extend AI Agents with Joule Studio

Today, we’re excited to share more details about Joule Studio, a new capability in SAP Build designed specifically for creating custom AI agents and Joule skills powered by Agentic AI. With Joule Studio, you can easily design, deploy, and manage intelligent agents uniquely tailored to your organization’s processes and data. It’s a significant step forward in enabling you to apply AI to tackle real-world challenges, streamline operations, and deliver tangible business impact.

“Joule Studio has helped us optimize resource allocation, improve time management, and enhance collaboration by using Agentic AI for our project management workflows. We have also used Joule Studio to enhance our AGIL AI.TimeManager application for time and activity management.”  
- Thomas Neuhaus, Head of Digital Experience Services, AGILITA Switzerland / Germany 

What is Joule Studio?

Traditional automation often falls short of addressing the complexity and dynamism of modern enterprises. While agents can think, reason, and adapt like skilled team members, they typically struggle with understanding business context and data, limiting their effectiveness across diverse landscapes.

Joule Studio, a capability of SAP Build, enables you to create purpose-built Joule agents and skills. It brings together SAP’s business process expertise and data from its comprehensive set of Cloud ERP applications with Agentic AI. Leveraging SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Knowledge Graph, these agents gain semantic access to business-relevant information across both SAP and non-SAP applications, empowering them to perform their tasks with greater context and precision. As part of SAP Build, agents can also interact with workflows, automations, and custom applications, and be embedded into your own solutions to extend and enhance business processes.

Here’s what you can build in Joule Studio with its drag-and-drop simplicity:

  • AI Agents that are capable of planning, reasoning, and executing complex business scenarios. Imagine an agent optimizing your logistics network, providing risk mitigation strategies, making procurement recommendations, and sourcing candidates for interviews—all while adapting to evolving requirements.
  • Joule Skills that are designed to handle repetitive, rule-based tasks, freeing your teams from routine activities such as data entry, IT ticket raising, and PO creation, thereby boosting productivity.

Both agents and skills are seamlessly orchestrated and triggered based on user intent and the context of conversations within Joule Copilot, delivering an intuitive end-user experience.


Image 1: Create an AI agent and Joule Skill project in SAP Build


Deeply Grounded in Business Process and Data: The SAP Advantage

One of the biggest challenges in enterprise AI is grounding agents in the context of your business. Many agents fail because they lack access to the correct data and don’t understand the nuances of your business. AI agents built with Joule Studio are deeply grounded in your business:

  • Data Grounding: Agents are anchored in your unique enterprise knowledge through SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Knowledge Graph. This ensures they understand not just the ‘what’ but also the ‘why’ behind every task, leading to more reliable outcomes.
  • Process Integration: Easily embed existing workflows, automations, integrations, and business logic from SAP Build into your agents and skills, streamlining cross-functional workflows.
  • Role Specialization: Tailor agents for specific business areas and roles, whether it’s finance, HR, supply chain, or customer service, so they align closely to your business needs.


Image 2: Provide instructions to the agent in natural language to define its goal and role


Open and Extensible Framework

Enterprises operate across multiple platforms and technologies. Joule Studio is designed to be open and extensible, allowing your AI agents to function across heterogeneous landscapes:

  • Connect to all your systems: Integrate your agents with external systems, APIs, and AI models, extending their reach beyond the SAP ecosystem. For instance, an agent could pull supply chain risk data from a third-party provider, analyze it, and offer supplier recommendations for procurement.
  • API Management and Governance: Use SAP Integration Suite to compose, govern, and monitor APIs exposed to agents and skills, including real-time usage analytics, anomaly detection, and predictive insights.
  • Flexible Model Choice: Choose from a range of out-of-the-box large language models or bring your own, ensuring your agents have the right reasoning and planning capabilities for your unique needs.


Image 3: Select from your existing action projects in SAP Build to connect to third-party systems

“Joule Studio in SAP Build is enabling teams to enrich Joule with client-specific skills and AI agents, seamlessly integrating it with the client’s application ecosystem, partner solutions, and agent network. This makes Joule a go-to solution for maximizing value and delivering a unified, agentic user experience.” 
- Stephanie Guimbellot, Global SAP Innovation & Sustainability Lead at Accenture 

Built-in Security and Compliance

Trust, transparency, and compliance are essential for AI agents. Joule Studio is built with these principles at its core:

  • Transparency: Gain complete visibility into agent planning, decision paths, and execution flows, so you can understand not just what your agents did, but why.
  • Centralized Lifecycle Management: Manage, deploy, and maintain all your agents and skills centrally within SAP Build, streamlining operations and updates.
  • Test and Validation: Test new agents and skills before deploying them into production, ensuring quality, reliability, and alignment with your business policies.
  • Compliance by Design: Ensure compliant interactions with AI using built-in data privacy controls and enforcement mechanisms.


Image 4: Test your agents and skills before deploying to minimize runtime errors.

Get a Closer Look at Joule Studio

Harness Agentic AI for your business with Joule Studio, empowering your teams to create AI agents and skills that accelerate innovation, boost productivity, and enhance decision-making. Custom Joule Skills will be generally available in June 2025 and custom AI Agents later in the year.


Want to learn more?

  • Register for the webinar: https://url.sap/2iuj2p
  • Take a guided product tour: https://url.sap/product-tour
  • Learn how to build Joule skills with this Interactive Value Journey: https://url.sap/skilldesign

entry.title: SAP Document AI — The Roadmap for Seamless Document Processing in SAP Business Suite
entry.content:
In today's digital world, efficient document processing has become an essential aspect of business operations. SAP Document AI is at the forefront of this transformation, providing innovative solutions that enhance productivity and streamline processes. Read more about the recent announcement here.

Upcoming Excellence with SAP Document AI Premium Plus 

Looking ahead, the SAP Document AI promises to simplify business document processing throughout SAP Business Suite through new, diverse and value-adding integration scenarios. For this purpose we plan to introduce the Premium Plus edition later this year. This edition harnesses the power of the latest generative AI technology, provides multi language support, offers turn-key integrations for enriched processing capabilities, instant learning capabilities, and harmonized experience across SAP’s portfolio.

 As part of the upcoming 2508 release of SAP Cloud ERP Public, the Premium Plus capabilities will enhance document efficiency with following embedded scenarios:

  • Quality Management: Automate the processing of incoming Supplier Quality Certificates to ensure quality compliance quickly and accurately.
  • Order Management: Expedite sales operations with automatic extraction of sales orders from documents in a variety of languages.
  • Inventory Management: Streamline inventory management by extracting key data from delivery notes for seamless tracking.

Users will benefit from end-to-end document processing automation, beginning with document ingestion, through AI processing, and culminating in matching with corresponding business objects within SAP Cloud ERP. All scenarios are also planned to be ported to the private edition soon [1].

For a hands-on look, watch the demo video depicting the processing of incoming supplier quality certificates in Quality Management:

Outlook and Roadmap for the entire SAP Business Suite

SAP’s vision for document processing integrates seamlessly into the current landscape and extends into the future. Future integrations of SAP Document AI include:

  • SAP SuccessFactors: Pre-configured content for high-volume HR documents like "National ID validation during New Hire Onboarding."
  • SAP Fieldglass: Efficiently upload and process Statements of Work within Services Procurement, simplifying document extraction and drafting.
  • SAP Enterprise Service Management: Specialized Document AI capabilities for business documents posting, including payment advice, into S/4HANA.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: Access to Generative AI capabilities for template-free document automation.
  • SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management: Enhanced invoice data extraction capabilities for superior invoice capture.

SAP Document AI continues to redefine document processing, offering businesses enhanced capabilities that drive efficiency, reduce errors, and empower organizations to focus on strategic tasks. Stay tuned and join our community of users on SAP Community. Stay updated on upcoming AI use case releases by exploring the SAP Roadmap Explorer. Explore more about SAP Document AI by visiting http://www.sap.com/ai/document-ai and don’t miss out on SAP's latest AI innovations at Sapphire 2025

 

Author's note:

1. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition users can already leverage SAP Document AI capabilities featuring Generative AI, notably by subscribing to and integrating with SAP Document AI Premium Edition via SAP BTP.

2. SAP Document AI, previously known as Document Information Extraction, has been available in variety of integrative scenarios across SAP as a "base" capability e.g. SAP Concur Invoice Management, SAP Cash Application, SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management and many more – check out this blog post for more details.

entry.title: SAP Document AI — Smarter, Faster, and More Efficient Document Processing
entry.content:
SAP Business AI is advancing its capabilities to seamlessly ingest and extract unstructured data from business documents, while automating document data handling integrated into SAP’s applications.

As part of Sapphire 2025 innovations we are excited to announce a transformative roadmap update for 2025. SAP is launching SAP Document AI, a complete business solution designed specifically for processing business documents. This development goes beyond merely rebranding; it is a refactoring of the product to align with market demands and expectations.

SAP Document AI will build upon the well-established Document Information Extraction Base and Premium editions, preserving their proven capabilities while enhancing efficiency. In addition, later in 2025 we plan to introduce the Premium Plus edition to expand the product's scope, ensuring smooth, out-of-the-box integration across SAP’s portfolio. For more details refer to this blog post. 

The Document Information Extraction name will be discontinued and all new innovations will be shipped as part of the SAP Document AI, offering a unified and comprehensive business document processing experience. Watch this short clip for a glimpse into the future of document processing with SAP:

Value Proposition

SAP Document AI offers comprehensive document processing for structured, semi-structured and unstructured data trapped within various document formats, unlocking its true potential for your business.

By seamlessly integrating and matching this data with your business information, SAP Document AI significantly reduces manual efforts and minimizes errors.

Leveraging the latest advancements in AI technologies, SAP Document AI provides enhanced accuracy, ensuring high-quality data for your organization. Our solution empowers informed decision-making while cutting processing costs, effectively lowering the total cost of ownership. All of this is delivered through seamless integration within SAP’s renowned business applications.

Our new solution promises to deliver significant benefits:

  • Accelerate productivity with automated and integrated workflows: Deliver fast time-to-value and higher productivity by automating data handling across a wide range of documents – all integrated directly into SAP applications
  • Enhance accuracy and quality of file handling with AI at scale: Improve quality and accuracy in document processing, minimizing errors and lowering the cost of ownership with state-of-the art AI at scale and reliable infrastructure.
  • Simplify operations with intuitive, secure experience: Reduce manual tasks and simplify processes with customizable, user-friendly experience, while ensuring robust security and compliance with industry leading privacy standards.

What to Expect - Capabilities and Innovations

Customers can look forward to an advanced solution that will provide a comprehensive suite of capabilities designed to maximize value:

  • Document Ingestion: Rapidly intake a wide variety of documents from your central email inboxes and cloud file storages, or upload ad-hoc through an intuitive user interface or SAP Mobile Start.
  • User-centric UX: An intuitive user experience designed for efficiency when working with and validating incoming documents, including value-adding Joule functionalities.
  • File Handling & OCR: Extract text and information accurately from documents in over 100 languages with advanced OCR capabilities out-of-the-box.
  • State-of-the-art AI Technology: Leverage a blend of latest AI technologies to ensure superior accuracy and adaptability, minimizing the need for extensive training data.
  • Pre-configured Content: Ready-to-use business content for operations like sales order processing, finance, quality management, logistics, employee onboarding, services procurement and many more.
  • Improvement Framework: Utilize an accuracy improvement framework enabling instant learning based on user corrections.
  • Extensibility & Configuration: Tailor your document processing system to your needs with extension & configuration features.
  • Globalization & Scaling: Scale your document processing operations globally with reliable infrastructure across hyperscalers and data centers.
  • Smooth Integration: Already embedded into a variety of business scenarios across SAP portfolio. Soon, also available via a standard interface for SAP Cloud ERP Public Edition and many more - check our roadmap outlook here.
  • Flexible Commercial Model: SAP Document AI remains consumable via BTPEA cloud credits for custom AI scenarios. Later in 2025, adoption is also possible for the holders of AI Units, offering immediate innovation opportunities from the outset of the SAP Business AI journey. This will be part of the Premium Plus plan.

Discover the potential of SAP Document AI and join our community of users on SAP Community. Stay updated on upcoming AI use case releases by exploring the SAP Roadmap Explorer. Explore more about SAP Document AI by visiting http://www.sap.com/ai/document-ai and don’t miss out on SAP's latest AI innovations at Sapphire 2025.

entry.title: How LeverX enhances CAD Data Search for Manufacturing with Joule Studio in SAP Build
entry.content:
LeverX creates intelligent CAD data discovery with Joule Studio in SAP Build

For more than 20 years, LeverX has been helping companies across industries implement, integrate, and customize SAP solutions to meet their unique business needs. Among our many focus areas is manufacturing, where efficient data management plays a crucial role in organizations’ day-to-day productivity.

As an early-stage adopter of Joule Studio, we had the opportunity to explore its capabilities in a project inspired by a semiconductor equipment manufacturer’s real-world problem  — and find out how Joule Studio could help overcome their data management challenges in engineering environments.

Why We Chose Joule Studio

Companies approach us with a wide range of challenges, and in every case, we aim to find innovative, effective solutions that not only solve our clients’  immediate problems but also help them reach their strategic goals.

When SAP introduced Joule Studio in SAP Build for early-stage exploration, we immediately saw an opportunity to address one of the persistent pain points in the manufacturing sector: managing and accessing technical data stored within CAD files.

At our client’s company, CAD engineers spent around 4 hours each week searching for technical data hidden in complex file structures, managing analytics, and organizing design files.

Additionally, around 30% of models (components) are created from scratch rather than utilizing existing components as a foundation, leading to extra time spent on design and potential delays in manufacturing and supply chain processes.

Without a guided design process — one that would pre-define attributes based on historical data and previous experience — overall design time increased by approximately 20%. This manual effort slowed down production, contributed to redundant design work, and affected project timelines, in general.

Although SAP systems handled structured data well, properties stored within CAD files were not easily searchable through standard SAP tools. This gap created an opportunity to test Joule Studio's ability to bridge structured and unstructured data.

Building the Solution

Having previous expertise with SAP Build and SAP BTP solutions, we were excited to be among the first to explore Joule Studio’s capabilities. Joule Studio builds on the familiar low-code and no-code tools of SAP Build, providing us with a streamlined and intuitive environment for developing custom Joule skills and AI Agents.

Custom Joule skills developed in Joule studio are natively designed to work with Joule's conversational AI interface, adapting to user intent effortlessly.

Thus, our goal was to develop a custom Joule  skill that would make it easier for engineers to search and retrieve hidden CAD data — enabling smarter, faster decisions directly within SAP Joule. 

Our solution combined several technologies:

  • DataLark acted as the data extractor and parser, pulling CAD file properties from storage into SAP BTP.
  • SAP AI Coreto manage the machine learning processes and ensure accurate parsing of CAD data
  • Joule Studio was used to create a custom Joule skill integrated into Joule.

To better understand the technical setup behind our solution, have a look at the architecture overview below:

Solution Architecture

The custom Joule skill allowed engineers to search and find CAD file properties that would otherwise remain hidden to standard SAP search tools. In our demo scenario, a Bill of Materials (BOM) Engineer used SAP Joule alongside the "Maintain Bill of Material" application in SAP S/4HANA to:

  1. Plan a BOM with related materials and parameters.
  2. Use the Joule assistant to locate necessary CAD models with the right attributes.
  3. Quickly create a complete and accurate BOM based on the search results.

 

With this setup, we projected potential savings of around 10% of time of each CAD engineer. Over time, this could contribute to faster project deliveries, fewer redundant designs, and more efficient supply chains.

Our Insights and Results

Throughout the project, we found that managing the variety and complexity of CAD file properties was a challenging but rewarding task. Since different models contained diverse property structures, developing a search mechanism that worked reliably across all cases required careful design.

Our solution demonstrated strong potential during early-stage testing. While some fine-tuning will be needed for broader production use, especially as Joule Studio evolves toward General Availability, the initial results 一 savings of up to 4 hours per week per CAD engineer 一 confirmed the value and innovation opportunities Joule Studio offers for unstructured data management in engineering environments.

Looking Ahead: Preparing for AI Agents and New Possibilities With Joule Studio

Even at this early stage, the project showed how Joule Studio could open new possibilities for managing unstructured data across both SAP and non-SAP landscapes. With its extensibility to third-party systems like CAD management tools, Joule Studio is well-equipped to deliver more comprehensive, cross-system intelligence and automation. Looking forward, Joule Studio’s upcoming AI Agent builder will unlock advanced capabilities. For instance, businesses will be able to deploy agents that collaborate intelligently across systems, automate multi-step tasks, and provide proactive recommendations—all while being grounded in a company’s unique data.

At LeverX, we are excited to continue exploring how Joule Studio and AI Agents can bring even more intelligence and automation into daily business processes — not only for CAD engineers but across many other roles that deal with complex, unstructured data.

Want to experience transformation like this at your company? Explore Joule Studio within SAP Build and see how you can drive automation and improve user productivity today!

entry.title: How Pricefx is transforming pricing software with Joule Studio in SAP Build
entry.content:
About Pricefx

At Pricefx, we specialize in pricing management and optimization software, serving industries like discrete manufacturing, process and chemical sectors, and distribution. Our customers range from mid-sized enterprises to global corporations, all seeking to streamline pricing strategies and boost profitability. Our projects typically involve integrating our pricing solutions with enterprise systems like SAP Sales Cloud to empower sales teams with actionable insights. While we hadn’t used SAP Build before, diving into Joule Studio as early-stage adopters was our first hands-on experience with SAP’s low-code tools, and we were impressed by its intuitive setup.

Why We Chose Joule Studio

AI Assistants and Copilots are reshaping how businesses operate, and at Pricefx, we’re no strangers to this trend. We’ve been developing our own Agentic AI Copilot to enhance our pricing software, focusing on delivering value through seamless integration with SAP’s ecosystem.

The use case we targeted was clear: empowering sales representatives with a daily dashboard where they could ask Joule questions like, “Which customers should I prioritize today?” or “What product recommendations fit this customer?” By connecting SAP Sales Cloud with Pricefx’s pricing engine through Joule Studio to Joule, we aimed to enable real-time recommendations, price suggestions, and negotiation guidance — helping drive better margins and more effective sales processes.

How We Used Joule Studio

Joule Studio opened up new possibilities for bringing Pricefx’s pricing knowledge with SAP’s AI landscape. A key challenge we faced was teaching Joule to properly understand pricing-related queries — so that it could recognize what the sales reps were asking and pull the right information from our systems. Joule Skills became the foundation for solving this. With custom Joule skills, we were able to define specific types of requests, map them to Pricefx’s data sources, and guide Joule to respond accurately based on the user’s intent.

Before building the Joule Skill, we first connected Pricefx’s pricing data to SAP Build Actions to make it API-consumable. This setup allowed Joule to easily access the necessary information when executing custom Joule skills, ensuring smooth and reliable data retrieval during conversations.

Out of the many Joule skills we explored during our early work with Joule Studio, one standout example was our product recommendation skill.

Pricefx Product Recommendation

 

This seamless interaction not only saves time but also ensures sales reps have data-driven suggestions at their fingertips. Developing this skill in Joule Studio was surprisingly efficient, thanks to its low-code environment, which let us focus on functionality rather than complex coding.

Looking Ahead with Joule Studio

Our experience with Joule Studio has reinforced our commitment to deepening our SAP partnership. The tool’s low-code approach aligns perfectly with our vision of self-service solutions, making it easier for users to leverage pricing insights without technical barriers. We were particularly impressed by how quickly we could build and deploy Joule skills, which speaks to the power of Joule Studio’s design-time.

In 2025 and beyond, we’re excited to take this further. With SAP’s AI Agents on the horizon in the very near future, we see huge potential to create end-to-end workflows — from insights to recommendations to actions. Our plan is to use Joule Studio to develop AI Agents that help our partners act on pricing data more effectively. This focus on actionable outcomes will continue to drive innovation in how pricing software integrates with daily operations.

We’re thrilled to be part of this journey and can’t wait to see where Joule Studio takes us next.

entry.title: How sovanta uses Joule Studio to take ticketing systems to the next level
entry.content:
Real-World Use Case with SAP Joule Studio

In an era where efficiency and intelligent automation are critical, SAP has introduced Joule – a powerful AI assistant that is redefining the way users interact with SAP systems. But what do real-world use cases look like when it comes to optimizing business processes and supporting employees across departments? As an early-stage adopter of Joule Studio, sovanta put the GenAI tool through its paces. From Extension to Integration to AI – sovanta has been consulting and supporting SAP customers in the cloud environment for 15 years and has developed into a specialized SAP BTP partner. Thanks to our experienced BTP, UX, AI, and Development experts, we have established ourselves as a reliable BTP implementation partner, focusing on innovative, easy-to-use and AI-powered business apps that semelssly integrate with various systems.

Bringing together our SAP and AI experience, we successfully implemented the first use case with Joule Studio. The result? An exciting use case in the Center domain. In this blog post, we’ll explore the background of that use case and how our clients can benefit from Joule going forward.

Joule Studio in SAP Build: What It Can Do

First things first: We took a deep dive into Joule Studio within SAP Build, and we’re excited by what we found. The combination of generative AI and deep integration into SAP systems already makes Joule a powerful tool for our clients. But the real magic happens with the addition of Custom Joule Skills via Joule Studio. These enable truly impactful use cases that can make a tangible difference for businesses.

A particularly interesting aspect – and challenge – is Joule’s chat history. Joule automatically logs conversations to autonomously select the appropriate skill for follow-up questions. However, it heavily depends on the descriptions of each skill to correctly trigger them. As Joule Studio pioneer, we observed that Joule occasionally struggles to trigger the most relevant skill for certain follow-ups. By fine-tuning our skill descriptions, we were able to significantly improve precision and context handling. We are excited to see that SAP is actively working to enhance Skill Matching capabilities, and partner feedback like ours is directly shaping the ongoing evolution of Joule Studio’s intelligence.

The Use Case: Ticketing System Integration

To fully explore Joule Studio’s potential, we developed a realistic use case focused on service centers. This scenario showcases how Joule can retrieve service case information by integrating with an external ticketing system and leveraging Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) for contextual insights. This allows users to instantly access relevant ticket data and receive solution recommendations based on similar past cases – even without relying on Joule’s Document Grounding capabilities, which were not yet available during our early adopter experience.

The Business Challenge

When a customer reports an issue with a product, service teams are confronted with a lot of challenges:

  • Understanding the specifics of the incoming service case, extracting key information from it
  • Quickly finding internal documents that may be relevant to the customer issue
  • Checking if a similar issue was previously reported or resolved

All of these actions require the service team to navigate between various different systems, having to copy and paste information and search terms across these.

The Solution and its Key Benefits

This is where the power of Joule Studio and its ability to connect Joule with various systems comes in handy. Making full use of custom skills, we connected:

  • A ticketing system (e.g., JIRA) via API to retrieve and update service tickets.
  • The sovanta GenAI DocumentChat via API to perform RAG using historical case data.
    Good to know: While native RAG orchestration via Document Grounding is still evolving, Joule Studio already makes it easy to integrate external RAG services through APIs as part of a custom Joule Skill. In our case, we demonstrated how external RAG can be connected— using the sovanta GenAI DocumentChat to bring additional context and insights into the conversation.

We allow Joule to interact with these systems via three separate Joule Skills:

  • Skill 1: Search Tickets: Allows Joule to search for specific Tickets in the ticketing System.
  • Skill 2: Find Past Solutions: Use RAG via a context in the Document Chat that contains past solutions.
  • Skill 3: Update Ticket: Allows Joule to update a specific ticket in the ticketing System.

The flow below shows how these skills work together in a real conversation.

Separating the new Joule skills helps make each step in the process more reliable and easier to control. By keeping actions like searching tickets, finding solutions, and updating cases separate, we make sure Joule picks the right skill depending on the user’s request. This approach also ties closely to the importance of good skill descriptions: when each skill is focused and clearly described, Joule can match follow-up questions much more accurately. It keeps the conversation flow smooth and gives us the flexibility to tweak or improve individual skills later without disrupting the whole setup.

This use case shows how a custom Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) service can already be effectively integrated with Joule Studio, even without native support. As a result, support teams benefit from context-aware, AI-generated responses that boost efficiency and improve the overall customer service experience. By instantly retrieving and analyzing ticket data, Joule enables significantly faster issue resolution. It also supports proactive problem-solving by suggesting solutions based on similar past cases.

Future of AI Agents in Joule Studio

Looking ahead, building AI Agents within Joule Studio will be a key next step. As participants in the SAP Alpha Program for AI Agents, we already see strong potential to extend our use case even further. In the future, AI Agents could completely take over first-level support activities such as autonomously requesting additional information, suggesting possible solutions and reacting to further feedback by customers.

This will also require handling ticket escalations efficiently. In many service centers, one common challenge is identifying when a support ticket needs to be escalated. Service agents often have to manually decide if an issue is urgent enough to involve higher-level support, which can lead to delays or missed escalations. With large ticket volumes, it’s easy for critical issues to get lost among routine cases. AI Agents in Joule Studio could help by proactively monitoring tickets for escalation triggers and forwarding those tickets to the correct second- or third level support experts — taking service center efficiency to the next level. 

Our Final Thoughts on Joule Studio

Joule Studio is proving to be a promising addition to the SAP ecosystem. With the ability to extend capabilities using Custom Skills and integrate external services like sovanta GenAI DocumentChat, it offers a flexible and future-proof way to enhance business processes. Integrating Joule with AI Agents will open up exciting possibilities for realizing advanced use cases within Joule Studio, taking full advantage of multi-skill orchestration and autonomous task planning and handling. At sovanta, we’re excited to continue exploring how our clients can benefit from SAP Joule – today and in the future.

entry.title: Developing Mobile Apps with SAP Build
entry.content:
A Comprehensive Blog Series on Mobile Application Development with SAP’s latest development tool set

Over the course of last year, I’ve published several blog posts covering SAP Build Apps and connectivity with other SAP systems. In those posts I’ve used various products available in the SAP Build portfolio, such as SAP Build Code to develop a CAP-based data service and SAP Build Work Zone to publish a web application created in SAP Build Apps.

This year, we have combined the SAP Build portfolio into a new commercial offering with a simplified licensing model to ease adoption. SAP Build is the unified SAP low-code, pro-code and AI assisted development platform designed to modernize enterprise software and embrace digital transformation.

In this new blog series, I will build upon the data service created last year and use various options related to SAP Mobile Services to create (mobile) applications. The blog posts are set up like a tutorial and will provide actionable insights and detailed steps to guide developers effectively.

Step-by-Step Guide to Mobile Application Development with MDK

The following sections offer a detailed walkthrough of the mobile app development process using SAP Build and Mobile Development Kit (MDK). Each step is designed to ensure precision and clarity, enabling developers to replicate the process seamlessly.

Many tutorials and demos make use of unauthenticated data sources to avoid the rather tedious process of making the connections work. This makes sense for quick demos, but does not reflect the reality we are in. In this series, I am going to show you what it is really like to use authentication and role-based access, and how to work around some quirks that are currently in the toolchain.

This year, I've switched to Drawio for creating the diagram.

Prerequisite: CAP-based Data Service

As mentioned, I am going to continue the blog series based on what I’ve published last year and reuse the data service. A quick recap:

In Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - Connectivity options for low-code development - part 1 I have created a very simple data model in SAP Business Application Studio containing a Product entity, added some sample data, and made sure only authenticated users were able to access it. In the following blog post Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - Connectivity options for low-code development - part 2 I have added more fine grained Role-based access control.

A few posts later, Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - refining data sources with CAP while keeping a clean core showcases using CAP to combine the Product entity with a subset of external data from an S/4HANA system: Supplier information from BusinessPartnerA2X.

And in the last relevant part, Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - adding application logic with CAP while keeping the core clean I have added custom application logic in the data service to manipulate some of the data coming from the S/4HANA system.

Creating a Mobile Project in SAP Build Lobby

The SAP Build Lobby serves as the central hub for project creation and management. We begin by selecting the option to Create a new application project.

Notice that the SAP Build Lobby’s user interface has been revamped.

After selecting the Objective – build an Application, click Next and select Mobile. We could also use the Full-Stack option, which we’ve also used to create, test and deploy our data service in the previous blog series. However, for this blog post I will use the dedicated Mobile development environment. This environment contains more tools related to mobile use cases.

After selecting Next, we have basically one choice: Mobile Application. This enables us to develop platform-agnostic, metadata-based applications that can be created once and deployed across multiple platforms.

After selecting Next, we can give the project a name and description and have the choice to select a Dev Space. You can create a new one or select an existing one – if available. Take note that you cannot have more than two Dev Spaces running simultaneously with SAP Business Application Studio. If you already have two Dev Spaces running, then please navigate to the Dev Space Manager via the link Additional Application Types and shut down at least one of them before creating a new mobile specific Dev Space.

Select Review to move to the next step in the project creation wizard.

The summary page gives you a clear overview of your selected choices for you to review. If all looks good, please go ahead and select Create.

You will navigate back to the SAP Build Lobby main page where you can see your project being created. This can take a few seconds to up to a minute, as the Dev Space is being created and spun up.

 

Accessing the Mobile Project in SAP Business Application Studio

Once the project is created, select the project name, which is a link to open SAP Business Application Studio with your mobile Dev Space.

A mobile development perspective is opened within SAP Business Application Studio, providing access to tools specifically tailored for mobile application development. This environment facilitates integration, editing, debugging, and deployment activities for your mobile application.

 

Creating a new mobile app configuration in SAP Mobile Services

In the Storyboard view of SAP Business Application Studio, initiate the process of creating a new Mobile Services application by selecting the + button in the Runtime Resources section.

If you have not signed into the Cloud Foundry runtime of your target environment yet, then you will be asked to sign in now. The easiest way to do this, is by using the SSO Passcode and opening a new browser page to generate the passcode. Copy the passcode and paste it into the field to sign in. All can be done with just a few clicks.

Make sure to use the Cloud Foundry Endpoint from your target runtime environment, as it may differ from your development environment. You can find the endpoint in the Overview tab of the target subaccount’s BTP cockpit.

After signing in, you’ll need to select the Cloud Foundry Organization and Cloud Foundry Space.

Once signed in and the target is selected, the SAP Mobile Services instance active in this environment will be used to create a new application configuration. If there already application configurations defined in this SAP Mobile Services instance, then they will be listed and you could select one (but we won’t …).

Select the + button to create a new mobile application configuration. We will provide an Application Name and Application ID and select IAS for the Security Settings. We can skip the sample OData ESPM, as we have created our own data service.

In the Next step, we will configure a Mobile Services specific destination that is associated with the mobile application configuration. Note that this is not a BTP destination that will be visible in the BTP Cockpit. You will rather find it in the Mobile Services Admin UI.

There are multiple ways to configure this destination. I will showcase two options:

  1. Make use of an existing BTP Destination.
  2. Configure an Internet Destination.

Configuring the mobile destination using an existing BTP Destination

Let's start with the easiest way by using an existing BTP Destination. 

We first provide a name for the destination. Then, select the Destination Type SAP Destination Service. The dropdown list for Cloud Destination Name will show all destinations defined in the subaccount. When we deployed the CAP service to BTP, a destination was created and configured for handling the authentication. By using the SAP Destination Service, we can make use of the same destination for the mobile services app. This saves us from having to configure the authentication.

After selecting the destination, we are done and select Create App.

Configuring the mobile destination as an Internet Destination

You can skip this part if the above worked for you. In case you have a data service, but there is no BTP Destination defined for accessing it, we can configure an Internet Destination. This is a bit more work to configure. I will use the same destination as reference here to configure it in this example. You can apply the same to connect to other OData services exposed to the internet. 

First, we provide a Destination Name.

Most of the remaining items can be copied from the BTP Destination that was already created during the deployment of the CAP service, except for the client secret.

In the screenshot below I have opened the destination deployed together with the data service. Items to be copied:

  • Destination Type: select Internet Destination.
  • URL: the URL at which we have exposed our data service.
  • SSO Mechanism: select OAuth2 User Token Exchange. This enables secure transmission of user identity (the "subject") between systems. This is especially useful in scenarios where a backend service (the "Sender") needs to act on behalf of a user when calling another service (the "Receiver").
  • Token Service URL: this is the endpoint where a client application sends a request to exchange credentials (like a client ID and secret) for an access token. This token is then used to authenticate subsequent API calls.
  • Token Service URL type: select Dedicated.
  • Client ID: This is a unique identifier assigned to an OAuth client (typically an application or service) that is registered with the Identity Authentication Service (IAS).

We have one item remaining that cannot be copied from the BTP destination: the client secret. It is hidden. Where can we find this secret? I’ll provide you one way to find it. I am sure there are more ways and maybe even easier way to get it (feedback is welcome 😉 ).

Navigate to the subaccount where you have deployed the data service and open the Cloud Foundry Space where the service is running. Check the Instances tab and select the uaa instance (named: <your service name>-uaa) deployed with your data service and select the link View Credentials. You will find the “clientsecret” in the JSON structure shown.

Once the form is completed, click the Create App button. The application configuration will now be created in SAP Mobile Services. This will take a few minutes. Once configured successfully, the state will change to Started.

However, before we continue adding the app to the project, we must fix and enhance the configuration. For this we need to navigate to the SAP Mobile Services Admin UI. You can easily open the correct instance by selecting the Mobile Services link.  

Fixing the Mobile Services Destination configuration

Now we need to resolve an issue with the destination settings. Navigate to the Connectivity settings.

As you can see, the destination that was created is not configured for Authentication. Select the destination and select Edit.

Ensure to configure the destination as described earlier. Luckily, most of the fields are already filled with the correct information. For the SSO Mechanism select OAuth2 User Token Exchange instead of No Authentication. Select Token Service URL Type : Dedicated. Click Next and Finish to save the destination configuration.

Setting Up Cross Context Single Sign-On (SSO)

Regardless of which method you've used to create the mobile destination, we need to activate one more feature for our app in SAP Mobile Services.

Navigate to the SAP Mobile Services Admin Cockpit to configure Cross Context Single Sign-On (SSO) for the newly defined mobile app. This feature in SAP Mobile Services allows users to reuse their OAuth tokens obtained in one context (like a desktop browser or mobile browser) in another context (like a native mobile app). This eliminates the need for users to log in again when switching between platforms. It allows for seamless user authentication across multiple backend systems. In our case, we have a CAP-based service deployed to BTP, that makes use of an external data source coming from an S/4HANA system.

In the application configuration, navigate to the Security settings.

Select the Edit button and tick Enable Cross Context SSO. Cross Context SSO streamlines user experiences by eliminating the need for multiple logins.

Click OK to update the Security settings and navigate back to the application’s Settings.

Validating the Destination Configuration

We can test the Destination within the SAP Mobile Services Admin Cockpit to verify its functionality and resolve potential issues before we even deploy the application to a mobile device. This ensures that the mobile application interacts seamlessly with backend systems, and we can avoid lengthy and troublesome debugging session on the mobile device.

When selecting Ping, the connection between SAP Mobile Services and the data source is checked. If this is not successful, then you need to check the URL you’ve specified.

Next to Ping, there is another button to open the OData Application Destination Test. Select this to open a simple UI that you can use to check the OData service.

You should be able to see entity data from Products, as well as entity data from MySupplier. If you do not see this data, then you’ll need to double check your configuration settings. There is no point adding the application to the project without access to this (meta)data, as the metadata is used to create the application’s data model and user interface structure.

Adding the App to the Project

Navigate back to SAP Business Application Studio, ensure the destination is selected and click the Add App to Project button.

Generating and Configuring a Passcode

This will initiate a passcode generation process. The passcode must be manually entered to validate access.

A notification in the bottom-right appears that helps you to open a hyperlink to create an SSO passcode.

Copy the passcode and go back to SAP Business Application Studio to paste the passcode into the prompted field at the top of the screen.

Checking the external resource’s data model

Once the application is added to the project you will see the Storyboard again, which is now updated with the newly added application and destination.

You can check the data model of the external resource by selecting the item and clicking View Diagram. This will open a graphical view of the mobile destination’s metadata, which is stored in an XML file. The model should be the same as what I’ve shown in the prerequisites section.

 

Creating the User Interface in Storyboard

Switch back to the Storyboard view and create a UI application. This will open a UI creation wizard to generate the MDK UI. You can change the application name if needed and select the MDK template type. In our case, we will go for the CRUD type, allowing users to create, read, update and delete entity data.

For the target MDK Client version, ensure the highest version is selected and enable auto-deployment to SAP Mobile Services after creation.

After selecting Next, on the second page of the wizard select Service Type OData. We do not want to enable mobile Offline capabilities because the data service we have created is not able to handle this.

We do want to select all data collections from the data source.

Select Finish. You will see an Action page prompting to overwrite the vscode settings. I think merging is the best option here (although you don't have that option).

After selecting Finish, the UI application is created and the application’s metadata bundle is deployed to SAP Mobile Services. Upon successful deployment, the MDK Application Editor is opened, showing an onboarding dialog containing a QR code.

Any SAP Mobile Services Client app can download the metadata from SAP Mobile Services after finishing the onboarding steps.

Loading and Previewing the Application

Now that the application has been deployed, we can use a so-called ‘player’ to ‘execute’ the combination of metadata and JavaScript on a device. It is possible to build such a player app yourself, customized to your needs. However, in most cases a generic client is good enough. We have published such a generic MDK client in the public app stores.

You can find the “SAP Mobile Services Client” in the Apple AppStore as well as the Google Play Store. Download and install this app on your device.

You can now scan the QR code with the Mobile Svcs app.

A short video of the mobile onboarding process can be seen below. Steps:

  1. Open SAP Mobile Services Client app
  2. Scan QR code
  3. Confirm onboarding URL
  4. Sign in to custom IdP
  5. Set up passcode
  6. Enable Face ID (for Apple devices)
  7. Client connects to SAP Mobile Services instance and checks for application metadata availability
  8. Confirm downloading app metadata update
  9. Run app

 

Conclusion

In this blog post I have outlined the steps to create a cross-platform mobile application in SAP Build starting from the SAP Build Lobby, all the way to having the application running on a mobile device. The mobile user of the app can access business data that is protected through authorization and role-based access control. 

This serves as a starting point to further application development steps.

As always, feedback is welcome to improve the blog posts, but most importantly the products involved.

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2505
entry.content:
SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2505

With the introduction of the new release 2505, this blog post presents a comprehensive overview of the latest features and enhancements. The release has been available since May 17th and serves as the successor to the 2502 release. Some highlights of the 2505 release include the reduction of the technical downtime for release upgrades, the quick Fiori app generation within ABAP Development Tools (ADT) and the AI-based RAP BO Generator. 

The list of all new features can be found on the What's New for SAP BTP ABAP environment page. 

All new AI features are summarized here.

 

ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

  • Working with the log when migrating CDS DDIC-based views to CDS view entities, after finishing the simulation or migration of CDS DDIC-based views to CDS view entities, you can now filter the log on the result page using the Show Objects drop-down list box 
  • Restructuring the "Code Completion" and "Source Code Editors" preference pages 
  • RAP Business Objects Test Double Framework: Supported EML Statements
  • Data preview now supports ‘between’ capabilities in the filter options
  • Testing web API-based services using Swagger UI
  • Creating SAP Fiori App using Quick Fiori Application Generator
  • Improvements in the ABAP coverage view, new features: Changing the coverage scope and Exclude from coverage 
  • ADT Links can now be incorporated in the HTML result of an IDE action, allowing users to navigate to development objects in ADT 
  • ABAP SQL Consumption Model, you can it to generate corresponding CDS external entities for each entity that is exposed in a SQL service 
  • Creating and editing CDS table entities 

Core Data Services (CDS)

  • Improved CDS Buffering, optimize the ABAP memory consumption for scenarios which make use of CDS Entity Buffering 

OData

  • OData UI Service from Scratch, you can create all RAP-service related repository objects from scratch by using the OData UI Service from Scratch wizard 
  • Null value handling, you can now find an overview of the differences in the behavior of null values and initial values in OData V2 and V4 with regard to sorting and filtering on the Fiori Elements UI as well as explicit filtering through OData Web API request 

Data Archiving

  • Storage Manager Implementation, you can now use a new interface method to rollback faulty verified archive files (details)

Application Jobs

  • New Skip Function, you can now skip a single execution of a periodic job chain in the Application Job Templates app (details)

Units of measurement

  • You can now retrieve the standard units of measurement and dimensions delivered by SAP using released ABAP Cloud APIs (CL_UOM*) (details)

Business Configuration App

  • New Email Configuration for the Upload Business Configuration App (details)
  • Intent Navigation, if all technical key fields are provided as parameters to uniquely identify a record of an object page or subobject page, it's opened directly (details)

XCO Library

  • XCO XSLX: You can now style the fonts of a cell of an Excel worksheet (details)

Print Queue Administrator Integration

  • You can now create and delete print queues directly using APIs without the need to enter the Maintain Print Queues app, you can do so using the communication scenario SAP_COM_0A86 (details)

IAM Information System

  • You can now display the relevant components for business catalogs and applications. This way you can check under which component you can create an incident if required (details)

Released APIs and software component relations

  • You can now read about the best practices of using released APIs and software component relations on the SAP Help Portal (details)

Provider Support Role

  • Include start authorizations for custom services in provider support role 

Development Test Role

  • The business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_EXT_TST for development test roles is now continuously extended, not only with the start authorizations for custom services, but also with full authorizations for any custom authorization object included in the authorization default values 

Technical Monitoring

  • Capture and download of PLV files in ABAP cross trace for SQL statement execution analysis (details)

Message Monitoring

  • Message Monitoring for Integration Experts, you can now download the original payload of your message (details)

Custom Code Analysis

  • 3-Tier Model ATC Checks, there are now two new ATC checks of category 3-Tier Model: "Usage of APIs" and "Allowed Enhancement Technologies" (details)

Landscape Portal

  • Monitoring customers and products, the new Customer Overview app in the Landscape Portal enables partners to keep track of their customers and products (details)

SAP Fiori Launchpad

  • Extend the Sort Priority Field in the Manage Launchpad Spaces App, you can now extend the sort priority field to +/- 9999.99999 (details)
  • Avatars for Themes in the User Settings (details)
  • Hide Empty Spaces and Pages in the SAP Fiori Launchpad (details)

SAPUI5

  • Referenced SAPUI5 Version, as part of hotfix collection 3,  SAPUI5 version 1.133.2 is used for the administrator’s SAP Fiori launchpad, SAP-delivered Fiori apps, and the custom-built SAP Fiori apps, which are deployed to the ABAP system (see upgrade windows and SAPUI5)

SAP HANA Cloud

  • Increased SAP HANA Cloud Backup Retention Time to 30 Days (details)

entry.title: Quo vadis PLM in SAP? Expert interview with Peter Vandrey (Cideon) and Benjamin Zorn (DSC)
entry.content:
SAP solutions for ERP and PLM have evolved significantly in recent years. For ERP, the focus is on the integration of SAP S/4HANA; the field of PLM has also been expanded with new possibilities and (cloud) functionalities. What does this mean for SAP customers? In this interview, Peter Vandrey (CIDEON Software & Services GmbH & Co. KG) and Benjamin Zorn (DSC Software AG), answer the most important questions.

 

How has SAP ERP evolved in recent years?

Peter Vandrey: "SAP has significantly enhanced the functions and integration options for ERP in the last years. The mere fact that there are now three deployment options for SAP S/4HANA – On-Premise, Private Cloud Edition, and Public Cloud Edition – shows that SAP is offering customers greater flexibility and new technical possibilities, also in the cloud. This is certainly triggered by the increased demands from the customer side to carry out certain processes in the engineering environment, in addition to pure design engineering. Especially in the phase of early engineering, functions from requirements management, idea management, or model-based systems engineering can now be covered much better. The further development of SAP Integrated Product Development running on Business Technology Platform (BTP) illustrates these efforts by SAP in the engineering field. Furthermore, the world is increasingly shifting towards cloud technologies, and, of course, the requirements of customers are changing as well, especially in engineering, when it comes to sharing information and collaborating in projects.”

Benjamin Zorn: "The trend towards cloud applications is clear, and SAP's focus on cloud technology is understandable and makes sense. Cloud-native applications offer more flexibility and facilitate scaling options nowadays. Furthermore, SAP uses this strategic orientation to seamlessly close gaps in the field of PLM. In the past, most customers only managed CAD data, but today, they want a comprehensive Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) solution. SAP sees PLMas an end-to-end process from the first idea to product discontinuation, and uses technological change to enrich and enhance functions.”

 

What advantages does a transition of the ERP system from SAP ECC or SAP R/3 to SAP S/4HANA offer?

Peter Vandrey: "Anyone who wants to stay technologically up-to-date should switch to SAP S/4HANA before long. SAP ECC is no longer being developed, and maintenance will end with the last extension option in 2033. Functional enhancements and new features are developed exclusively for SAP S/4HANA. Also hybrid scenarios and individual solutions tailored to the customer’s needs will only be available for SAP S/4HANA. In the field of PLM, we can facilitate this transition, for example, by switching the engineering environment to SAP S/4HANA before the main update of the ERP system. This way, we simplify the main ERP transition.”

Benjamin Zorn: "SAP S/4HANA offers several technological advantages over SAP ECC. For example, the database technology is completely different. The new in-memory database technology offers significant improvements in terms of performance. In the field of Artificial Intelligence (AI), SAP S/4HANA has already the AI assistant Joule, and this field will certainly grow in the future, also in engineering. It is important for customers to create the data foundation today in order to benefit from these innovations in the future.”

 

Many customers hesitate when it comes to the cloud. What advantages does cloud deployment have over on-premise systems?

Benjamin Zorn: "The great advantage of cloud technology is that the entire operation of the system is outsourced. This way, the customer does not need any internal resources for system hosting. Expenditures for hardware, installation, and maintenance are eliminated with native cloud systems and cloud hosting. Especially in light of the skilled labor shortage, the often scarce resources in the IT department can be utilized more effectively. Cloud providers, such as SAP, have these resources available and take care of your system’s maintenance on a daily basis. Even if many companies are still skeptical today and do not want to give away their product data, which they consider to be the “treasure” of their company: Is the data really more secure lying on private servers in companies with limited resources and tight budgets, which makes it even harder to handle the data with the same care and professionalism? In some industries, data must not be stored in the cloud for legal reasons, but we advise all other companies to consider cloud deployment options, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private or Public Edition.”

Peter Vandrey: "We’ve seen some of our customers being offline for half a year because they were hacked and their data was encrypted. The likelihood of that happening with a professional cloud data center is significantly lower. These data centers are certified and operate according to defined standards. There has been a significant mindset shift in the last years. Some software manufacturers and also customers who initially rejected the cloud outright are increasingly opening up to the new possibilities. Of course, data security and data privacy continue to be of the utmost priority.”

 

Which SAP S/4HANA deployment option is the best fit for which companies?

Peter Vandrey: "The smaller the company, the more the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition pays off. Especially for companies with few employees in IT, external cloud hosting is profitable, and the existing functionalities in the public cloud come in handy. Additionally, it is the most cost-effective setup of SAP S/4HANA. An on-premise installation and the private cloud are significantly more complex in terms of setup and maintenance, but on the other hand, they offer more possibilities. It really depends on the company and its size. Many customers are opting for hybrid scenarios. For example, the parent company uses the private cloud, while the public cloud is being implemented at the acquired subsidiary or at another location. This is also called a two-tier approach.”

Why are hybrid scenarios perfect for transitioning to the cloud?

Benjamin Zorn: “For us, hybrid scenarios are the best way to meet individual customer needs and gradually implement more and more cloud functions. This allows subsidiaries, individual locations, or single business areas, such as engineering, to gradually shift towards cloud applications, while the parent company or the traditional ERP area still uses the original on-premise installation. Especially discrete manufacturers come with individual setups and adjustments that should be sensibly adapted in the new environment – and this is made possible by a hybrid approach. This way, companies maintain their innovative strength and make use of the latest technology while continuing to use their well-established processes.”

Peter Vandrey: "In the last approximately 20 years, we have built a wide range of functions for engineering on-premise, which cannot just be transferred one-to-one to the cloud overnight. Certain cloud solutions may initially be of interest to smaller companies. But for customers who want to take the step to the cloud already today, we also offer functionalities which will continue to evolve in the next years. The important thing is, that the engineering department can continue to use the tired-and-true solutions and that the data is exchanged securely with SAP S/4HANA. Especially with hybrid scenarios, we offer the best of both worlds and such separate introduction projects are interesting for many companies in terms of costs and technologies."

 

Which PLM functionality is actually necessary for a manufacturing company?

Peter Vandrey: "The requirements for PLM are usually resulting from the field of engineering. The design department works with one or more CAD systems, the PLM environment must be compatible with the required functionalities, for example, for data reuse or the collaboration with manufacturing and service. There are many pieces that need to come together. At this point, it is important to seek advice, for example, from experienced software manufacturers and consulting firms like Cideon or DSC, who have been working with SAP for decades.”

 

What PLM functions does SAP Integrated Product Development running on SAP BTP already offer today?

Benjamin Zorn: "SAP Integrated Product Development takes PLM to the next level. In the past, SAP mainly concentrated on the management of product data, focusing on PDM, while with SAP Integrated Product Development, additional PLM functions are coming to the forefront. This opens up fields such as idea management, innovation management, requirements management, and many more. SAP Integrated Product Development is a native cloud solution which also offers companies a secure space for collaboration with third parties. Especially in the two-tier approach, when a company uses more than one deployment option of SAP S/4HANA, this solution provides future opportunities for an extended use of PLM functionality.”

 

What role does SAP ECTR play in the context of new developments such as SAP Integrated Product Development and SAP BTP?

Benjamin Zorn: "For hybrid scenarios, SAP Engineering Control Center (SAP ECTR) is the absolute key component because it bridges the gap between the classic and the cloud-native world. SAP ECTR can display functions from SAP Integrated Product Development. For users, there are no changes: they can continue to work in their familiar environment and still use cloud functions such as requirements management.”

Peter Vandrey: "Even for customers who want to use SAP Fiori instead of SAP GUI, SAP ECTR makes it possible to manage the navigation accordingly. From SAP ECTR, users can access the SAP Fiori interface or SAP Integrated Product Development. These connections are increasingly developed, allowing customers to switch to modern user interfaces with the latest capabilities.”

 

What role does SAP CAD Desktop still play, which product is an alternative for customers, and what is important for making this transition successfully?

Peter Vandrey: "SAP CAD Desktop no longer plays a relevant role; the support for this product will also end in 2033 at the latest. Customers who are currently still using SAP R/3 and SAP CAD Desktop and are considering a transition should switch to SAP ECTR. SAP ECTR also works with SAP R/3, facilitating the actual ERP transition when companies have previously made the transition from SAP CAD Desktop to SAP ECTR. Therefore, customers should definitely keep data migration in mind. Customers transitioning from SAP CAD Desktop to SAP ECTR only need to make minor adjustments, such as data enrichment. In general, the data from SAP CAD Desktop is usable, and it is easier to stay within the SAP environment than to switch to a solution from a different manufacturer. This switch would include all aspects of a data export from SAP and a complete import of all CAD data into the new (non-SAP) system. In our daily work, we experience that especially data import is one of the most time-consuming and labor-intensive project steps. This step and the familiarization with new data handling for everyone involved can be skipped with SAP ECTR."

Benjamin Zorn: “For users of SAP CAD Desktop, SAP ECTR is the product that already meets the most requirements out of the box. Therefore, it is straightforward to switch from SAP CAD Desktop to SAP ECTR."

 

What is the partnership between Cideon and DSC like in day-to-day business and in customer projects?

Peter Vandrey: "Cideon and DSC have been working together for a long time. We already collaborated on customer projects in the late 1990s and early 2000s. With the introduction of SAP ECTR, the official partnership has been established. This cooperation has many advantages for our customers. We can cover a wide technological range together and with the help of additional partners. With this expertise, we take a look at the customer’s individual needs and then design the system landscape accordingly. The customer's requirements are decisive, not the technology. We work together very closely, developing joint ideas that become products and solutions, providing manufacturing companies with real value added."

Benjamin Zorn: "With extensive know-how in the field of CAD and many different CAD systems, Cideon is very well positioned. We at DSC provide the strategic integration platform with SAP ECTR, which lays the foundation upon which various components can be built. The communication in customer projects is seamless, the customer requirement is always our focus, and we are a well-functioning team. It is well known in the partner network that we can cover a wide range in the fields of engineering, manufacturing, and mechatronics.”

 

We recently released a joint webcast on PLM in SAP. Why is it a must-see?

Benjamin Zorn: “In the webcast, we provide a detailed overview of the current and future opportunities about PLM in the SAP environment. As an SAP customer, it is extremely important to learn what is now possible with PLM software from SAP. We give an assessment so that customers can better gauge their current technological status. Many companies are facing a transition to SAP S/4HANA and should be aware of all PLM options in order to make informed decisions today and in the future.”

Peter Vandrey: "The webcast provides a comprehensive and clear overview in just 45 minutes. Anyone who wants to know more about the PLM possibilities in SAP can contact us directly."

Watch our free webcast "Quo vadis, PLM in SAP?" now. Find guidance, use solutions: How to profit from the PLM portfolio of SAP and learn everything you need to know about PLM in SAP.

This interview was originally published by Cideon Software & Services GmbH.

entry.title: Fusion Forward: Unlocking Innovation with SAP Build – Series 1: What is Fusion Development?
entry.content:
In this post, you’ll learn how fusion development combines the strengths of citizen and professional developers using SAP Build and SAP Business Technology Platform to create fast, cost-effective, and business-aligned applications—covering the collaborative process, real-world examples, and how to get started with this innovative approach.

What is Fusion Development?

Fusion development is a game-changing approach that blends the expertise of professional developers (who code complex logic and ensure system scalability) with citizen developers (business users who understand processes but don’t code). By combining these strengths, fusion development creates applications that are both technically robust and perfectly aligned with business goals.

At its core, fusion development is about collaboration. It bridges the gap between IT and business teams, enabling them to work together seamlessly. With SAP Build, a low-code platform, citizen developers use drag-and-drop tools to design user interfaces, while professional developers handle integrations and backend logic using SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). The result? Custom applications delivered faster, at lower cost, and with greater relevance to business needs.

 

Real-World Analogy: Think of fusion development as a culinary collaboration. Citizen developers choose the ingredients—defining what the dish (app) should look like and do—while professional developers cook it, ensuring it’s perfectly seasoned and structurally sound. Together, they create a masterpiece that satisfies everyone.

Why Fusion Development Matters

In today’s fast-paced world, businesses need agile solutions to stay competitive. Fusion development delivers by:

  • Empowering Everyone: Non-coders, like store managers or finance teams, can create apps, reducing reliance on IT.
  • Accelerating Delivery: Apps that once took months can now be built in weeks, thanks to low-code tools.
  • Enhancing Collaboration: IT and business teams work hand-in-hand, ensuring solutions meet both technical and practical needs.
  • Maximizing Investments: For SAP customers, fusion development extends existing systems like SAP S/4HANA without costly customizations.

Example in Action: Imagine a retail chain needing an app to track inventory during a holiday rush. A store manager uses SAP Build Apps to design a simple barcode-scanning interface, while an IT developer integrates it with SAP S/4HANA for real-time updates. In just weeks, the app is live, streamlining operations and boosting efficiency.

The Power of SAP Build

SAP Build is the engine behind fusion development, offering a suite of tools designed for collaboration:

  • SAP Build Apps: Enables citizen developers to create intuitive mobile or web interfaces without coding.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: Automates workflows, like approvals or notifications, to streamline processes.
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Provides professional developers with tools for backend development, integrations, and scalability.

These tools work together to create a seamless development experience. For example, a citizen developer might build a customer feedback app, while an IT developer connects it to SAP systems for data analysis—all within the same platform.

Benefits of Fusion Development

Fusion development offers transformative advantages:

  • Speed: A logistics firm built a delivery tracking app in two weeks, compared to months with traditional methods.
  • Accessibility: Retail managers create inventory apps, freeing IT for strategic projects.
  • Innovation: A startup prototyped a customer feedback app in days, adapting quickly to market needs.
  • Cost Efficiency: Pre-built templates in SAP Build cut development costs significantly.

What’s Next in the Series?

This is just the beginning! In the next posts, we’ll explore:

  • Real-world case studies, like automating invoice processing.
  • Step-by-step guides to building your own fusion development apps.

Get Started with Fusion Development

Fusion development is more than a trend—it’s a new way to innovate. With SAP Build, anyone can contribute to building powerful, business-driven applications. Ready to dive in? Join the SAP Builders community, explore SAP Build tutorials, or share your thoughts in the comments below. Let’s unlock innovation together!

Call to Action: Stay tuned for Series 2, where we’ll dive into a real-world example of fusion development in action. follow for updates!

Conclusion:
Fusion development is revolutionizing how businesses create applications by uniting the creativity of citizen developers with the expertise of professional developers. Powered by tools like SAP Build and SAP Business Technology Platform, this collaborative approach delivers faster, cost-effective, and business-aligned solutions. From empowering non-coders to accelerating innovation, fusion development enables organizations to stay agile and competitive in a rapidly evolving world. As we continue this series, get ready to explore real-world success stories and practical steps to harness the power of fusion development. Join the SAP Builders community today and start building the future of business applications!

Please feel free to share your thoughts in the comments section below.

If you enjoyed reading this blog post and would like to receive similar content in the future, please consider following my profile. By following my profile @gaddamarunkumar , you will receive updates on new blog posts and other content related to ABAP development best practices.

Follow the SAP BTP ABAP environment Topic page: SAP BTP

This page is a great resource for staying up-to-date on the latest news and developments in the SAP ABAP environment. You can follow the page to receive updates, post and answer questions, and read other posts on the topic

entry.title: Troubleshooting Guide for Known Issues in SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog, I’ll walk you through six common issues encountered in SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard along with step-by-step solutions.

 

Applicable Versions

This troubleshooting guide applies to specific versions only. The issues described in this article are known and will be resolved in the upcoming version. Please stay tuned for the next release.

SAP Analytics Cloud Versions: 1.0.0 and 1.1.0

SAP Datasphere Versions: 1.0.0 and 1.1.1

 

Issue 1 Story Management Error

Symptom

There are alerts marked with exclamation mark icons in the drop-down menu of Add New Data from the toolbar in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Procedure

  1. Open SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard in SAP Analytics Cloud.
  2. Open edit mode.
  3. From the toolbar, select Add New Data > [The Model to be Replaced] > Replace....
  4. If a warning pops up, choose Continue.
  5. In the Select Model dialog, select Select other model... > SAPDWC in the Datasphere section > [Your Workspace Name] > [Model Name] (should match the name of the model to be replaced).
  6. Choose OK.
  7. In the Replace Model panel, choose Preview > Replace Model. For more information, see Replace Models in Stories (Optimized) | SAP Help Portal.
  8. From the toolbar, select Add New Data > [The Model to be Replaced] > Remove to remove the replaced model.
  9. Repeat steps 3 to 8 until all analytic models are replaced.

 

Issue 2 Incorrect CO2e-Flow Statement

Applicable Version

SAP Datasphere Applicable Version: 1.0.0

This issue doesn't occur in the latest version. We recommend upgrading your system to the latest version to resolve the problem.

Symptom

The opening and closing data of CO2e flow statement in SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard isn't correct.

For more information about SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard, see SAP Green Ledger Overview | SAP Help Portal.

Procedure

  1. Download the ZIP file here.
  2. Extract the ZIP file.
  3. In SAP Datasphere, open the Repository Explorer.
  4. Above the result table, choose Import > Import Objects from CSN/JSON File.
  5. Select a space that you want to import.
  6. Select and import the JSON file you just downloaded.
  7. Select all objects with status Ready to Import. Exclude objects marked as Already in the Repository.
  8. Choose Import CSN File.
  9. Once finished, select the imported objects and choose Deploy.
  10. In the Repository Explorer,  search for the business name "Co2e Flow Statement Union (HL)" and open it.
  11. In line 28, replace "OpenTranToCustomers" with "FiscalYearPeriod".
  12. In lines 33 and 66, replace "SAP_GRL_HL_EmissionSumTranToCustomers" with "SAP_GRL_HL_EmissionSumTranToCustomersWOP".
  13. In line 61, replace "CloseTranToCustomers" with "FiscalYearPeriod".
  14. Choose Save and Deploy.

 

Issue 3 Currency or Unit Conversion Error

Symptom

A currency/unit conversion error message occurs while previewing the green ledger analytic model in SAP Datasphere.

Procedure

In SAP Datasphere, search for the technical name "SAP_GRL_HL_JrnlEntrEmissionLdngLdgrUntCnvrsn" in the Repository Explorer.

  • If no results are found, perform the following steps.
    1. Download the ZIP file here.
    2. Extract the ZIP file.
    3. Choose Import > Import Objects from CSN/JSON File.
    4. Select and import the JSON file you just downloaded to space "SAP_CONTENT".
    5. Select all objects with status Ready to Import. Exclude objects marked as Already in the Repository.
    6. Choose Import CSN File.
